_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-29 22:54:58Z)**_

The changed file is a self-contained React mockup (`docs/vercel_mockup/src/MOCKUP.jsx`) — explicitly marked at line 24 as "Not live. No data persists." It is a static prototype with in-memory state, no backend, no auth boundary, and no remote data flow.

Analysis of the added block (lines added under `StaffWalkinScreen`):

- **Data source**: `govHospitalBookings` is local React state passed via props from the same in-process mockup.
- **Rendering sinks**: All booking fields (`b.ref`, `b.passenger.name`, `b.voyageTime`, `b.class`, `b.seat`, `b.agency`, `b.rejectionReason`) are rendered as React text children, which React auto-escapes — no `dangerouslySetInnerHTML`, no `href`, no `src`, no template/HTML string concatenation.
- **Authorization**: `mine = govHospitalBookings.filter((b) => b.officer === staff.name)` is presentational filtering inside a UI mockup; there is no privileged operation on the server side to bypass.
- **State mutation**: `cancelBooking` invokes `setGovHospitalBookings` to filter local state by `ref`. No network call, no persistence, no IPC.
- **Style props**: All `style` values are static constants from `COLORS` / hard-coded literals (no user-controlled URLs, no `url(...)` interpolation).

No source→sink path with security impact exists in the diff. No findings.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-29 22:55:05Z)**_

No security findings. The change is to a static UI mockup (`docs/vercel_mockup/src/MOCKUP.jsx`, declared "Not live. No data persists.") that filters in-memory React state by officer name and renders booking fields as auto-escaped React text children. No backend, no real authorization boundary, no injection sink (no `dangerouslySetInnerHTML`, no URL/href interpolation, no `eval`, no shell/SQL/path operations). The "Cancel request" handler only mutates local component state.
